recipes.title: Pupusas
recipes.description: Salvadoran national dish: thick handmade corn tortillas stuffed with cheese, beans or chicharrón and griddled.
steps: Mix masa harina, salt and warm water into a soft, pliable dough; rest 10 minutes.
Take a ball of dough, hollow it with your thumb, fill with cheese (and beans, chicharrón or loroco), and seal.
Flatten between oiled hands into a 1 cm thick disc.
Cook on a hot, lightly oiled comal for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and slightly puffed.
Serve immediately with curtido and salsa roja.

recipes.title: Curtido
recipes.description: Salvadoran lightly fermented cabbage, carrot and onion slaw, the obligatory companion to pupusas.
steps: Place cabbage, carrot and onion in a heatproof bowl and pour boiling water over to wilt slightly; drain after 1 minute.
Stir in oregano, salt, crushed red pepper and vinegar.
Pack into a jar and press down so the brine covers the vegetables.
Cover loosely and rest at room temperature 24 hours, then refrigerate.

recipes.title: Yuca Frita
recipes.description: Salvadoran fried cassava served with curtido and chicharrón or fried fish, a classic merendero plate.
steps: Boil yuca in salted water for 25 minutes until just tender, then drain and remove the woody core.
Pat dry and let cool slightly.
Heat oil to 180°C and fry yuca chunks until golden and crisp.
Pile onto a banana leaf, top with chicharrón, blanket with curtido and finish with tomato sauce.

recipes.title: Sopa de Pata
recipes.description: Salvadoran rich beef-foot and tripe soup with yuca, plantain and corn, a market-day favourite.

ingredients.name: corn on the cob, in rounds

steps: Place cow's foot and tripe in a large pot with onion, garlic and water; simmer 3 hours skimming foam.
Remove the meats, dice the tripe and return everything to the broth.
Add yuca, carrot and corn; cook 15 minutes.
Add plantain and cabbage and cook 10 minutes more until tender.
Season with salt and finish with cilantro just before serving with lime wedges.

recipes.title: Tamales Pisques
recipes.description: Salvadoran masa tamales with refried beans rolled into the centre, wrapped in plantain leaves.
steps: Beat masa harina, lard, salt and warm liquid into a fluffy, spreadable masa.
Pass plantain leaves over a flame to soften, then cut into 25 cm squares.
Spread a generous layer of masa on each leaf, top with a strip of refried beans down the centre.
Fold the masa over the beans so they're enclosed, then fold the leaf into a flat parcel and tie.
Boil tamales upright in salted water for 60 minutes; rest 10 minutes before unwrapping.

recipes.title: Pastelitos de Carne
recipes.description: Salvadoran fried corn-masa half-moon pies stuffed with seasoned ground beef and potato, a beloved street snack.
steps: Sauté onion, garlic and potato until soft, add beef, cumin, tomato sauce and salt; cook 10 minutes and cool.
Mix masa harina, salt, achiote oil and warm water into a soft dough; rest 10 minutes.
Press a ball of dough flat between plastic, fill with a spoon of picadillo, fold to a half-moon and seal edges.
Heat oil to 180°C and fry pastelitos 4-5 minutes until crisp and golden.
Drain and serve with curtido and tomato salsa.

recipes.title: Pan con Pollo
recipes.description: Salvadoran chicken sandwich with watercress, cucumber, radish and a tomato-pumpkin-seed sauce.

ingredients.name: watercress, lettuce, cucumber, radish for garnish

steps: Roast tomatoes, onion and garlic until charred, then blend with pumpkin seeds, sesame, achiote and a cup of water.
Brown chicken in oil, add the sauce and Worcestershire, cover and simmer 35 minutes.
Pull the chicken from the bone and return to the sauce; adjust seasoning.
Split the rolls, soak the inside lightly with the warm sauce, then fill with chicken and the fresh vegetables.
Serve immediately, the bread soft and saucy.

recipes.title: Sopa de Res
recipes.description: Salvadoran beef soup with corn, plantain and root vegetables in a clear, herbaceous broth.

ingredients.name: corn on the cob, in rounds

steps: Simmer beef shank with onion, salt and water for 90 minutes, skimming foam.
Add yuca, carrot and corn and cook 15 minutes.
Add chayote, plantain and potato and cook 10 minutes more.
Finish with cabbage and cook 5 minutes; stir in cilantro and mint and adjust seasoning.
Serve with rice, lime and chilli on the side.

recipes.title: Salpicón
recipes.description: Salvadoran finely chopped beef salad with mint, radish, onion and lime juice, served with rice and tortillas.
steps: Chop the cooked beef as finely as possible by hand.
Combine beef with radish, onion, mint and cilantro.
Dress with lime juice, olive oil, salt and pepper.
Rest 10 minutes for flavours to come together, then serve cool with rice, tortillas and lime wedges.
